---
title: "COLORECTAL-GTEx STRATEGY"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# LCP1-NET: WGCNA Pipeline — Strategy 3
# GTEx-only normal reference with covariate regression
#
# Key differences from Strategies 1 and 2:
#   - Normal reference built from GTEx colon ONLY (~700 samples)
#   - TCGA matched normals removed from training data entirely
#   - Known biological covariates regressed out within GTEx
#     before WGCNA (age, sex, RNA integrity)
#   - TCGA normals held out as validation group
#   - TCGA tumours scored as test group
#
# This produces a three-way anomaly comparison:
#   GTEx normals (training baseline)
#   TCGA normals (held-out — captures field cancerisation)
#   TCGA tumours (test — captures tumour network dysregulation)
#
# Scientific rationale:
#   TCGA matched normals are collected adjacent to active tumours
#   from cancer patients. Field cancerisation, systemic inflammation,
#   and tumour microenvironment signals mean these samples are not
#   truly normal. Including them in the normal reference dilutes
#   the anomaly signal and biases co-expression module detection.
#   GTEx colon samples from healthy volunteers represent the
#   cleanest available normal colorectal tissue reference.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

library(ggplot2)
library(jsonlite)
library(limma)
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
    library(recount3)
    library(DESeq2)
    library(WGCNA)
    library(flashClust)
    library(SummarizedExperiment)
    library(gridExtra)
})

allowWGCNAThreads()

# ── Paths ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
BASE_PATH_S3 <- "~/Desktop/COLORECTAL CANCER/LCP1-NET/strategy3"

dir.create(file.path(BASE_PATH_S3, "data/wgcna"),     recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(BASE_PATH_S3, "data/processed"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(BASE_PATH_S3, "ml_outputs"),     recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(BASE_PATH_S3, "plots"),          recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

LCP1_ENSEMBL <- "ENSG00000159111"

cat("Strategy 3 environment ready ✓\n")
cat(rep("=", 60), "\n", sep="")
```

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOCK 1 — DATA ACQUISITION
# Reload from Strategy 1 save if availablE
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("\nBLOCK 1: Data Acquisition\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")

s1_raw_path <- "~/Desktop/COLORECTAL CANCER/LCP1-NET/all_raw_counts.RData"

if (file.exists(s1_raw_path)) {
    cat("Reloading raw counts from Strategy 1 save...\n")
    load(s1_raw_path)
    cat("Loaded successfully ✓\n")
    cat("  counts_colon:       ", ncol(counts_colon),       "samples\n")
    cat("  normal_coad_counts: ", ncol(normal_coad_counts), "samples\n")
    cat("  normal_read_counts: ", ncol(normal_read_counts), "samples\n")
    cat("  tumour_coad_counts: ", ncol(tumour_coad_counts), "samples\n")
    cat("  tumour_read_counts: ", ncol(tumour_read_counts), "samples\n")

} else {
    cat("Strategy 1 save not found — downloading...\n")

    set_recount3_url <- function() {
        urls <- c(
            "https://recount-opendata.s3.amazonaws.com/recount3/release",
            "https://sciserver.org/public-data/recount3/data",
            "http://duffel.rail.bio/recount3"
        )
        for (url in urls) {
            cat("Trying:", url, "... ")
            tryCatch({
                options(recount3_url = url)
                test <- available_projects()
                cat("OK ✓\n")
                return(invisible(test))
            }, error = function(e) cat("FAILED\n"))
        }
        stop("All recount3 URLs failed.")
    }

    human_projects <- set_recount3_url()

    # GTEx Colon
    rse_colon    <- create_rse(subset(human_projects,
        project == "COLON" & file_source == "gtex"))
    counts_colon <- assay(rse_colon, "raw_counts")
    meta_colon   <- colData(rse_colon)

    # TCGA-COAD
    rse_coad  <- create_rse(subset(human_projects,
        project == "COAD" & file_source == "tcga" &
        project_type == "data_sources"))
    counts_coad <- assay(rse_coad, "raw_counts")
    meta_coad   <- colData(rse_coad)

    barcodes_coad      <- meta_coad$tcga.tcga_barcode
    type_coad          <- substr(barcodes_coad, 14, 15)
    tumour_coad_counts <- counts_coad[, type_coad == "01"]
    normal_coad_counts <- counts_coad[, type_coad == "11"]

    # TCGA-READ
    rse_read  <- create_rse(subset(human_projects,
        project == "READ" & file_source == "tcga" &
        project_type == "data_sources"))
    counts_read <- assay(rse_read, "raw_counts")
    meta_read   <- colData(rse_read)

    barcodes_read      <- meta_read$tcga.tcga_barcode
    type_read          <- substr(barcodes_read, 14, 15)
    tumour_read_counts <- counts_read[, type_read == "01"]
    normal_read_counts <- counts_read[, type_read == "11"]

    save(counts_colon,
         normal_coad_counts, normal_read_counts,
         tumour_coad_counts, tumour_read_counts,
         meta_colon, meta_coad, meta_read,
         file = s1_raw_path)
    cat("Raw data saved ✓\n")
}

n_gtex <- ncol(counts_colon)
cat("\nBlock 1 complete ✓\n")
```

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOCK 2 — GENE ID HARMONISATION
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("\nBLOCK 2: Gene ID Harmonisation\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")

strip_version <- function(ids) sub("\\..*", "", ids)

rownames(counts_colon)       <- strip_version(rownames(counts_colon))
rownames(normal_coad_counts) <- strip_version(rownames(normal_coad_counts))
rownames(normal_read_counts) <- strip_version(rownames(normal_read_counts))
rownames(tumour_coad_counts) <- strip_version(rownames(tumour_coad_counts))
rownames(tumour_read_counts) <- strip_version(rownames(tumour_read_counts))

# Find common genes across all five matrices
# Even though TCGA normals are not used for training,
# we still need common genes for tumour projection later
common_genes <- Reduce(intersect, list(
    rownames(counts_colon),
    rownames(normal_coad_counts),
    rownames(normal_read_counts),
    rownames(tumour_coad_counts),
    rownames(tumour_read_counts)
))
cat("Common genes across all sources:", length(common_genes), "\n")

counts_colon         <- counts_colon[common_genes, ]
normal_coad_counts   <- normal_coad_counts[common_genes, ]
normal_read_counts   <- normal_read_counts[common_genes, ]
tumour_coad_counts   <- tumour_coad_counts[common_genes, ]
tumour_read_counts   <- tumour_read_counts[common_genes, ]

cat("LCP1 in common genes:", LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% common_genes, "\n")
cat("Block 2 complete ✓\n")
```

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOCK 3 — GTEx-ONLY NORMAL REFERENCE
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Strategy 3 uses GTEx colon ONLY as the normal reference.
# TCGA matched normals are deliberately excluded from training.
#
# They are preserved separately for:
#   1. Held-out validation (three-way anomaly comparison)
#   2. Demonstrating field cancerisation effect in Results

cat("\nBLOCK 3: GTEx-Only Normal Reference\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")

# Normal reference = GTEx colon only
normal_gtex <- counts_colon

cat("Normal reference (GTEx only):", ncol(normal_gtex), "samples\n")
cat("Genes:", nrow(normal_gtex), "\n")

# Store TCGA normals separately for later validation
tcga_normal_combined <- cbind(normal_coad_counts, normal_read_counts)
tcga_normal_labels   <- c(
    rep("TCGA_COAD_Normal", ncol(normal_coad_counts)),
    rep("TCGA_READ_Normal", ncol(normal_read_counts))
)

cat("\nTCGA normals held out for validation:\n")
cat("  TCGA-COAD normal:", ncol(normal_coad_counts), "samples\n")
cat("  TCGA-READ normal:", ncol(normal_read_counts), "samples\n")
cat("  Total held-out:  ", ncol(tcga_normal_combined), "samples\n")
cat("\nThree-way comparison will be:\n")
cat("  Group 1: GTEx normals (training baseline)\n")
cat("  Group 2: TCGA normals (held-out validation)\n")
cat("  Group 3: TCGA tumours (test anomaly group)\n")
cat("Block 3 complete ✓\n")
```

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOCK 4 — QUALITY CONTROL
# Applied to GTEx samples only
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("\nBLOCK 4: Quality Control (GTEx only)\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")

# ── Expression filter ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 20% threshold on GTEx samples only
# ~700 GTEx samples → threshold ~140 samples
min_samples_threshold <- ceiling(ncol(normal_gtex) * 0.20)
keep_expressed        <- rowSums(normal_gtex > 10) >= min_samples_threshold

cat("Genes before filtering:", nrow(normal_gtex), "\n")
cat("20% threshold (GTEx):  ", min_samples_threshold, "samples\n")
cat("Genes passing filter:  ", sum(keep_expressed), "\n")

# Check LCP1
lcp1_pass <- LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% rownames(normal_gtex)[keep_expressed]
cat("LCP1 at 20% threshold: ", lcp1_pass, "\n")

if (!lcp1_pass) {
    cat("Lowering to 10%...\n")
    min_samples_threshold <- ceiling(ncol(normal_gtex) * 0.10)
    keep_expressed        <- rowSums(normal_gtex > 10) >= min_samples_threshold
    cat("Genes at 10%:", sum(keep_expressed), "\n")
    cat("LCP1 at 10%: ", LCP1_ENSEMBL %in%
        rownames(normal_gtex)[keep_expressed], "\n")
}

# ── Variance filter ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Top 15,000 most variable genes within GTEx
expressed_gtex <- rownames(normal_gtex)[keep_expressed]
gene_var_gtex  <- apply(normal_gtex[expressed_gtex, ], 1, var)
top_var_flag   <- rank(-gene_var_gtex) <= 15000

# Force LCP1 inclusion
if (LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% names(gene_var_gtex)) {
    top_var_flag[names(gene_var_gtex) == LCP1_ENSEMBL] <- TRUE
    cat("LCP1 forced into gene set ✓\n")
}

keep_final_genes <- expressed_gtex[top_var_flag]
normal_gtex_filtered <- normal_gtex[keep_final_genes, ]

cat("\nGenes after variance filter:", length(keep_final_genes), "\n")
cat("LCP1 in final set:          ",
    LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% rownames(normal_gtex_filtered), "\n")

# ── Library size QC ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
lib_sizes         <- colSums(normal_gtex_filtered)
low_lib_flag      <- lib_sizes < quantile(lib_sizes, 0.05)

cat("\nGTEx library size summary:\n")
print(summary(lib_sizes))

if (any(low_lib_flag)) {
    cat("Removing", sum(low_lib_flag), "low-quality GTEx samples\n")
    normal_gtex_filtered <- normal_gtex_filtered[, !low_lib_flag]
    cat("GTEx samples remaining:", ncol(normal_gtex_filtered), "\n")
} else {
    cat("All GTEx samples pass library size QC ✓\n")
}

cat("\nFinal GTEx reference:\n")
cat("  Samples:", ncol(normal_gtex_filtered), "\n")
cat("  Genes:  ", nrow(normal_gtex_filtered), "\n")
cat("  LCP1 present:",
    LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% rownames(normal_gtex_filtered), "\n")
cat("Block 4 complete ✓\n")
```

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FIX A — CLEAN GTEx COUNTS BEFORE VST
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("\nFix A: Cleaning GTEx counts...\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")

n_na    <- sum(is.na(normal_gtex_filtered))
n_large <- sum(normal_gtex_filtered > .Machine$integer.max, na.rm = TRUE)
n_neg   <- sum(normal_gtex_filtered < 0, na.rm = TRUE)

cat("NA values:            ", n_na,    "\n")
cat("Values > integer max: ", n_large, "\n")
cat("Negative values:      ", n_neg,   "\n")

normal_gtex_clean <- normal_gtex_filtered

normal_gtex_clean[
    normal_gtex_clean > .Machine$integer.max] <- .Machine$integer.max
normal_gtex_clean[!is.finite(normal_gtex_clean)] <- 0
normal_gtex_clean[is.na(normal_gtex_clean)]      <- 0
normal_gtex_clean[normal_gtex_clean < 0]         <- 0
normal_gtex_clean <- round(normal_gtex_clean)
storage.mode(normal_gtex_clean) <- "integer"

cat("After cleaning:\n")
cat("  NA values:    ", sum(is.na(normal_gtex_clean)),    "\n")
cat("  Negatives:    ", sum(normal_gtex_clean < 0),       "\n")
cat("  Storage mode: ", storage.mode(normal_gtex_clean),  "\n")
cat("Fix A complete ✓\n")
```

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOCK 5 — VST NORMALISATION (GTEx only)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# No batch correction needed here — all samples are from GTEx
# processed through the same Monorail pipeline.
# We regress out biological covariates (age, sex, RIN) instead.

cat("\nBLOCK 5: VST Normalisation\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")

# No batch labels needed — single source
col_data_gtex <- data.frame(
    sample_id = colnames(normal_gtex_clean),
    row.names = colnames(normal_gtex_clean)
)

dds_gtex <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
    countData = normal_gtex_clean,
    colData   = col_data_gtex,
    design    = ~1
)

cat("Applying VST to GTEx-only matrix...\n")
vst_gtex    <- vst(dds_gtex, blind = TRUE)
expr_gtex   <- assay(vst_gtex)

cat("VST complete ✓\n")
cat("Dimensions:", nrow(expr_gtex), "genes ×",
    ncol(expr_gtex), "samples\n")
cat("Value range: [",
    round(min(expr_gtex), 2), ",",
    round(max(expr_gtex), 2), "]\n")
cat("Block 5 complete ✓\n")
```

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOCK 6 — COVARIATE REGRESSION WITHIN GTEx
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Since all samples are from GTEx, we have no inter-batch effects
# to correct. Instead we regress out known biological and technical
# covariates that drive within-GTEx variance:
#
#   RNA integrity score (SMRIN) — technical quality variable
#   Donor age                  — biological covariate
#   Donor sex                  — biological covariate
#
# This reduces the within-GTEx PC1 variance and produces cleaner
# co-expression modules that reflect shared tissue biology
# rather than donor-to-donor demographic differences.
#
# Only the covariate effect is removed — the underlying
# gene expression variance used for WGCNA is preserved.

cat("\nBLOCK 6: Covariate Regression Within GTEx\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")

# ── Inspect available GTEx metadata columns ───────────────────────────
cat("Available GTEx metadata columns (subset):\n")
meta_cols <- colnames(meta_colon)
print(head(meta_cols, 40))

# Common GTEx metadata column names in recount3:
# gtex.smrin   — RNA integrity number (RIN)
# gtex.sex     — donor sex (1 = male, 2 = female)
# gtex.age     — donor age range (e.g. "50-59")
# gtex.smtsd   — tissue subsite (e.g. "Colon - Sigmoid")
# gtex.smcenter— collection center

# Filter metadata to samples that survived QC
# (normal_gtex_clean column names are the surviving samples)
gtex_meta_filtered <- as.data.frame(meta_colon)[
    colnames(normal_gtex_clean), ]

cat("\nSample counts in filtered metadata:",
    nrow(gtex_meta_filtered), "\n")

# ── PCA before covariate regression ──────────────────────────────────
pca_before_cov <- prcomp(t(expr_gtex), scale. = FALSE)
var_before_cov <- pca_before_cov$sdev^2 /
                  sum(pca_before_cov$sdev^2) * 100

# Colour by available metadata for diagnosis
if ("gtex.sex" %in% meta_cols) {
    sex_labels <- as.factor(gtex_meta_filtered$gtex.sex)
} else {
    sex_labels <- rep("unknown", ncol(expr_gtex))
}

pca_before_df <- data.frame(
    PC1 = pca_before_cov$x[, 1],
    PC2 = pca_before_cov$x[, 2],
    sex = sex_labels
)

p_before_cov <- ggplot(pca_before_df,
                       aes(PC1, PC2, colour = sex)) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
    labs(
        title    = "Strategy 3 — GTEx PCA before covariate regression",
        subtitle = paste0("PC1 = ", round(var_before_cov[1], 1),
                          "% — coloured by donor sex"),
        x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_before_cov[1], 1), "%)"),
        y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_before_cov[2], 1), "%)")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_before_cov)
cat("PC1 variance before covariate regression:",
    round(var_before_cov[1], 1), "%\n")

# ── Build covariate matrix ────────────────────────────────────────────
# Only include covariates that actually exist in your metadata
# Check each before using

covariates_available <- c()
covariate_matrix     <- NULL

# RNA integrity score
if ("gtex.smrin" %in% meta_cols) {
    rin_vals <- as.numeric(gtex_meta_filtered$gtex.smrin)
    if (sum(!is.na(rin_vals)) > ncol(expr_gtex) * 0.8) {
        rin_vals[is.na(rin_vals)] <- median(rin_vals, na.rm = TRUE)
        covariate_matrix <- cbind(covariate_matrix, RIN = rin_vals)
        covariates_available <- c(covariates_available, "RNA integrity (RIN)")
        cat("Added covariate: RNA integrity (RIN)\n")
    }
}

# Donor sex
if ("gtex.sex" %in% meta_cols) {
    sex_vals <- as.numeric(gtex_meta_filtered$gtex.sex)
    if (sum(!is.na(sex_vals)) > ncol(expr_gtex) * 0.8) {
        sex_vals[is.na(sex_vals)] <- 1
        covariate_matrix <- cbind(covariate_matrix, sex = sex_vals)
        covariates_available <- c(covariates_available, "donor sex")
        cat("Added covariate: donor sex\n")
    }
}

# Donor age — GTEx stores as range (e.g. "50-59")
# Convert to numeric midpoint
if ("gtex.age" %in% meta_cols) {
    age_raw  <- gtex_meta_filtered$gtex.age
    age_mid  <- sapply(strsplit(as.character(age_raw), "-"),
                       function(x) mean(as.numeric(x)))
    if (sum(!is.na(age_mid)) > ncol(expr_gtex) * 0.8) {
        age_mid[is.na(age_mid)] <- median(age_mid, na.rm = TRUE)
        covariate_matrix <- cbind(covariate_matrix, age = age_mid)
        covariates_available <- c(covariates_available, "donor age")
        cat("Added covariate: donor age\n")
    }
}

# Tissue collection site (center)
if ("gtex.smcenter" %in% meta_cols) {
    center_vals <- as.numeric(factor(gtex_meta_filtered$gtex.smcenter))
    covariate_matrix <- cbind(covariate_matrix, center = center_vals)
    covariates_available <- c(covariates_available, "collection center")
    cat("Added covariate: collection center\n")
}

if (is.null(covariate_matrix) || length(covariates_available) == 0) {
    cat("\nWARNING: No covariates found in metadata\n")
    cat("Check column names above and update accordingly\n")
    cat("Proceeding without covariate regression\n")
    cat("expr_corrected_s3 = expr_gtex (no regression applied)\n")
    expr_corrected_s3 <- expr_gtex

} else {
    cat("\nCovariates being regressed out:\n")
    for (cov in covariates_available) cat(" -", cov, "\n")

    # ── Apply covariate regression via limma ──────────────────────────
    # removeBatchEffect with covariates argument removes the linear
    # effect of each covariate while preserving expression variance
    cat("\nApplying limma covariate regression...\n")

    expr_corrected_s3 <- removeBatchEffect(
        expr_gtex,
        covariates = covariate_matrix
    )

    cat("Covariate regression complete ✓\n")
    cat("Value range after regression: [",
        round(min(expr_corrected_s3), 2), ",",
        round(max(expr_corrected_s3), 2), "]\n")
}

# ── PCA after covariate regression ───────────────────────────────────
pca_after_cov <- prcomp(t(expr_corrected_s3), scale. = FALSE)
var_after_cov <- pca_after_cov$sdev^2 /
                 sum(pca_after_cov$sdev^2) * 100

pca_after_df <- data.frame(
    PC1 = pca_after_cov$x[, 1],
    PC2 = pca_after_cov$x[, 2],
    sex = sex_labels
)

p_after_cov <- ggplot(pca_after_df,
                      aes(PC1, PC2, colour = sex)) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
    labs(
        title    = "Strategy 3 — GTEx PCA after covariate regression",
        subtitle = paste0("PC1 = ", round(var_after_cov[1], 1),
                          "% — sex effect should be reduced"),
        x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_after_cov[1], 1), "%)"),
        y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_after_cov[2], 1), "%)")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_after_cov)

# Save covariate regression PCA
pdf(file.path(BASE_PATH_S3, "plots/covariate_regression_PCA.pdf"),
    width = 16, height = 7)
grid.arrange(p_before_cov, p_after_cov, ncol = 2)
dev.off()
cat("PCA plots saved\n")

cat("\nPC1 variance summary:\n")
cat("  Before covariate regression:", round(var_before_cov[1], 1), "%\n")
cat("  After covariate regression: ", round(var_after_cov[1], 1), "%\n")
cat("  Reduction:                  ",
    round(var_before_cov[1] - var_after_cov[1], 1), "%\n")

cat("Block 6 complete ✓\n")
cat("expr_corrected_s3 is ready for WGCNA\n")
```

```{r}
# ── Environment diagnostic ────────────────────────────────────────────
# Run this to see exactly what variables exist in your session

cat("All objects currently in your R environment:\n")
print(ls())

cat("\nLooking for expression-related objects:\n")
expr_objects <- ls()[grep("expr|corrected|gtex|vst|normal",
                           ls(), ignore.case = TRUE)]
print(expr_objects)

cat("\nLooking for PCA objects:\n")
pca_objects <- ls()[grep("pca", ls(), ignore.case = TRUE)]
print(pca_objects)
```

```{r}
# ── Check 1: Sample outlier detection ────────────────────────────────
# pca_after_cov already exists from Block 6 — no changes needed here

pca_scores   <- pca_after_cov$x[, 1:2]

pc1_outliers <- abs(scale(pca_scores[, 1])) > 3
pc2_outliers <- abs(scale(pca_scores[, 2])) > 3
outlier_flag <- pc1_outliers | pc2_outliers

cat("Samples flagged as potential outliers:\n")
cat("  PC1 outliers:", sum(pc1_outliers), "\n")
cat("  PC2 outliers:", sum(pc2_outliers), "\n")
cat("  Total unique:", sum(outlier_flag), "\n")

if (any(outlier_flag)) {
    cat("\nOutlier sample IDs:\n")
    print(rownames(pca_scores)[outlier_flag])
    cat("These will be caught and removed by cutreeStatic in Block 7\n")
} else {
    cat("No extreme outliers detected ✓\n")
}
```

```{r}
# ── Check 2: Scale-free topology pre-check ──────────────────────────
cat("expr_corrected_s3 dimensions:", dim(expr_corrected_s3), "\n")

# Transpose for WGCNA format (rows = samples, columns = genes)
expression.data.s3 <- t(expr_corrected_s3)
cat("Expression matrix (samples x genes):", dim(expression.data.s3), "\n")

# Scale-free topology check at power = 6
cat("\nRunning scale-free connectivity check...\n")
k <- softConnectivity(
    datExpr = expression.data.s3,
    power   = 6,
    type    = "signed"
)

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
scaleFreePlot(k,
    main = "Scale-free topology check (power = 6)\nStraight line = data suitable for WGCNA")

cat("Check complete\n")
cat("Straight line on log-log scale = proceed to Block 7 ✓\n")
cat("Strong curve = flag for supervisor before proceeding\n")
```
